note: 2017–2019 confirmed from Facebook group archive. 2020 partial (meeting confirmed Apr 9, outcomes in Sleeper). 2021 DogeFAAB confirmed from league context. 2025 confirmed from Google Doc transcript (May 28, 2025). 2022–2024 still need Commissioner transcripts.

meetings.summary: The DogeFAAB era hits its first major governance test: MCSchools/Escuelas left the league without cashing out FAAB. The 2026 meeting determines what happens to orphan FAAB, how new owners are onboarded into the DOGE system, and whether the annual refresh happens before or after the dispersal draft.
meetings.context: MCSchools left before 2026 season. New owner search underway. Roster 9 stewarded by Bimflé (AI). First dispersal draft in league history pending. 2026 Rookie Draft order locked.

propositions.title: Orphan FAAB Disposition — what happens to MCSchools' remaining FAAB?
propositions.description: Options: new owner inherits the balance, FAAB reverts to league pool, or FAAB is forfeited.

propositions.title: New Owner Starting FAAB
propositions.description: How much FAAB does a new owner start with? Options: standard balance, prorated, league median, or fresh 0.

propositions.title: Dispersal Teams — FAAB Eligibility
propositions.description: Teams receiving dispersal draft picks: are they eligible to use FAAB immediately, or is there a restriction window?

propositions.title: Annual Refresh Timing
propositions.description: Should the 2026 annual FAAB refresh buy window open before or after the dispersal draft? Affects how new owner's cap is set.

propositions.title: Refresh Buy Caps — New Owner and Dispersal Teams
propositions.description: What refresh cap limits apply to the new owner and teams receiving dispersal assets?

meetings.summary: Six propositions voted on. Major outcomes: QB roster capped at 4, roster cut by 2 more spots (to 26/22), and the loser punishment expanded to three selectable options — including the Moody Mile, the Wax, and the Golf Skirt. Treasury diversification (Trump coin/meme coins) and unlimited FAAB purchases both failed. Heading into Season 10.
meetings.context: MLSchools12 won Season 9 (2024 champion). MCSchools still in the league. Meeting chaired by Commissioner Chris Rioux. Attendees included Schoolcraft, DeLaura, Eldridge, Vieyra, Maleski, Cogdeill, and others. Chris Rioux's brother attended via proxy.

propositions.title: Quarterback Roster Limit — Max 4 QBs
propositions.description: Teams limited to a maximum of four quarterbacks on the active roster (excluding taxi squad and injured reserve). Designed to free up roster spots in free agency.

propositions.title: Starting Lineup Changes — Reduce RBs, Add Flex
propositions.description: Proposed reducing required starting RBs from two to one and adding flex positions. Michael Schoolcraft opposed, citing experience in another league where 'running backs were worthless.'

propositions.title: Last Place Punishment — Selectable Options
propositions.description: Replaced the mandatory Moody Mile with three selectable punishments: (1) Run the Moody Mile, (2) Wax hairiest body part (excluding head/face), (3) Golf 18 holes in a skirt.

propositions.title: Treasury Diversification — Meme Coins / Collectibles
propositions.description: Proposed diversifying DogeFAAB treasury from Dogecoin into Trump coin, meme coins, or sports card collectibles. Failed to pass.

propositions.title: Roster Size Reduction — Cut 2 Spots
propositions.description: Cut two roster spots: offseason roster reduced from 28 to 26, regular season active roster from 24 to 22.

propositions.title: Remove FAAB Refresh Cap — Unlimited Purchases
propositions.description: Matthew DeLaura proposed unlimited FAAB refresh purchases ('print the money'). Commissioner Rioux opposed, explaining the current cap prevents inflation based on prior spending history.

meetings.context: Heading into Season 9. JuicyBussy was defending champion (2023).
Heading into Season 8. Grandes was defending champion (2022) — Commissioner's ring year.
Heading into Season 7. MLSchools12 was defending champion (2021). DogeFAAB in its first full season.

meetings.summary: The meeting that created the DogeFAAB system — FAAB backed by Dogecoin held in Commissioner escrow. Owners can buy FAAB via annual refresh window at a fixed rate (5 FAAB = 1 DOGE). This fundamentally changed the economic stakes of waiver wire bidding.
meetings.context: Heading into Season 6. Cogdeill11 was defending champion (2020). League on Sleeper for its second full season. Rising crypto interest provided the creative spark for the DogeFAAB concept.

propositions.title: Introduce DogeFAAB System
propositions.description: Back all FAAB with Dogecoin held in Commissioner escrow. Annual refresh buy window lets owners purchase FAAB at a fixed rate. FAAB carries over year to year.

meetings.summary: First virtual-only meeting due to COVID-19. Rule change proposals were posted in Sleeper rather than Facebook. Known outcomes: Matt Moodie was replaced by Joe Tuberdyke (Tubes94), completing a roster of active owners for the new Sleeper era. Full proposition list and votes not yet recovered from archive.
meetings.context: COVID year. League had migrated from ESPN to Sleeper in December 2019 — Season 5 would be the first fully on Sleeper. Schedule-related proposals from 2019 (Props F and G) were carried over to this meeting for re-vote.

propositions.title: Schedule Rule Changes (carried from 2019 Props F & G)
propositions.description: Schedule/division-related proposals from the 2019 meeting that showed interest but needed a platform transition to implement. Specific terms not yet recovered from archive.

meetings.summary: Landmark governance year: the Taxi Squad was created, a formal loser punishment was established, and free agents were made eligible for the Rookie Draft. The controversial anti-trade rape rule (Prop I) was defeated — Commissioner Grandes declared he'd 'beat any trade that is offered to you. *probably.'
meetings.context: Heading into Season 4 on ESPN. Cogdeill11 defending champion (2018). Dan Gaudy still in league (would be replaced by Escuelas around this time). Google Meet virtual meeting with full 11-owner rollcall.

propositions.title: Unknown Proposition
propositions.description: Proposition A details not recovered from archive.

propositions.title: Unknown Proposition
propositions.description: Proposition B details not recovered from archive.

propositions.title: Unknown Proposition
propositions.description: Proposition C details not recovered from archive.

propositions.title: Taxi Squad
propositions.description: Each team may designate 2 roster spots as taxi squad, for rookies only. Players may be activated off taxi during the season but cannot return to taxi unless dropped and re-claimed via waivers. Owners cannot claim players directly off another team's taxi squad.

propositions.title: Annual Loser Punishment
propositions.description: The team finishing last place (defined annually) must perform a punishment set before the season start. In 2019: last place in the regular season had to complete a Beer Mile — video evidence required before the 2020 Rookie Draft.

propositions.title: Schedule Change (details TBD)
propositions.description: Schedule-related proposal that showed interest but needed further details. Deferred for re-vote at the 2020 meeting.

propositions.title: Schedule Change (details TBD)
propositions.description: Second schedule-related proposal deferred from 2019 for re-vote at the 2020 meeting after platform migration.

propositions.title: Free Agents Eligible in Rookie Draft
propositions.description: Allow free agent rookies (undrafted) to be selected in the Rookie Draft using standard draft picks. Previously only already-rostered rookies could be traded; this change dramatically improved undrafted rookie value.

propositions.title: Anti-Trade Rape Rule
propositions.description: Proposed protection against exploitative one-sided trades — would have given the Commissioner or league broader power to block or penalize egregious trades.

meetings.summary: The most transformative single meeting in league history: IDP removed, Superflex added, roster sizes cut, dues raised to $50, a loser punishment codified, trade restrictions on future picks enacted, and trades during the playoffs banned. Eight owners attended in person in Cleveland; four dialed in.
meetings.context: Heading into Season 3 on ESPN. MLSchools12 (Schoolcraft) won the inaugural championship (2016) and was defending champion after MLSchools' 2017 title. Meeting held in-person at Commissioner Grandes' home in Cleveland.

propositions.title: Remove IDP Players
propositions.description: Individual Defensive Players eliminated from all rosters. Roster management had become too complex and stat corrections from IDP were creating ongoing disputes.

propositions.title: Roster Size Reduction
propositions.description: Active roster cut from 28 to 24 players (offseason roster from 32 to 28). Designed to tighten the talent pool and make waiver wire more competitive.

propositions.title: Superflex Position Added
propositions.description: A Superflex starting spot (QB/RB/WR/TE eligible) was added to lineups, dramatically increasing QB value in trades and the Rookie Draft.

propositions.title: Annual Dues Increase to $50
propositions.description: Entry fee raised from $40 to $50 per year. $40 was not generating a large enough prize pool to sustain engagement.

propositions.title: Future Pick Trade Restriction
propositions.description: Trading future draft picks requires the trading team to pay 50% of the annual dues for every year prior to and including the year of the traded pick. Prevents owners from trading away picks for years they haven't yet paid for.

propositions.title: Loser Punishment Established
propositions.description: The team with the lowest total potential (optimal) points scored through the end of the playoffs is designated as the loser and must perform a punishment determined before the season starts.

propositions.title: No Trades During Playoffs
propositions.description: Trading prohibited beginning the last regular season week through the end of the playoffs (deadline: Thursday after the final regular season week at 12:00pm).

meetings.summary: The first Owners Meeting — a call-in / WebEx session with 9 of 12 owners (Moodie, Drysdale, and Gaudy absent). Three scoring changes passed immediately. A wave of offseason rule change proposals was submitted in December 2017 for the 2018 meeting.
meetings.context: Heading into Season 2 on ESPN. MLSchools12 (Michael Schoolcraft) was defending inaugural champion (2016). The league was still finding its governance rhythm — this was the first formal meeting, dialed into 1-800-504-8071 with passcode 7530014#.

propositions.title: Passes Defended: +1 Point
propositions.description: Added 1 point for each pass defended by an IDP player. Effective immediately for the 2017 season.

propositions.title: Sacks: 2 → 3 Points
propositions.description: Increased the point value of a sack from 2 to 3 points. Effective immediately for the 2017 season.

propositions.title: Interceptions: 2 → 4 Points
propositions.description: Increased the point value of a defensive interception from 2 to 4 points. Effective immediately for the 2017 season.
